title: Figure_Supplementary 2
author: Tahmasebi, Ghazaal
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Supplementary Figure 2. Firing rate estimation optimization | (A)  Treadmill protocol schematic with temporal scale windows overlaid. Scales ≥ 30 s extend across rest–locomotion boundaries and were excluded from selection.  (B)  Mean silhouette score as a function of temporal scale for four firing rate estimation methods. The causal exponential filter at τ = 10 s (star) was selected over the marginally higher-scoring Gaussian (silhouette 0.314 vs 0.371) because both recovered the same k = 2 cluster structure and the exponential filter preserves temporal causality.  (C) Top row:  example normalized firing-rate trajectories for the dominant cluster under each method across temporal scales.  Bottom row: corresponding normalized kernels, illustrating symmetric (rectangular, square-root–rectangular, Gaussian) versus causal (exponential) weighting in time.
